title: Gentianella pygmaea
intro: Gentianella pygmaea, also known as pygmy gentian, is a small, perennial plant that belongs to the gentian family (Gentianaceae) and is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Gentianella pygmaea include iridoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Gentianella pygmaea is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to treat digestive issues, coughs, and colds.
taxonomy: Gentianella pygmaea is classified within the family Gentianaceae. It belongs to the genus Gentianella, which is a group of small to medium-sized annual and perennial plants. The species is further classified as a member of the order Gentianales and the class Magnoliopsida. Gentianella pygmaea is native to the mountainous regions of Europe, particularly in the Alps and the Carpathian Mountains. This classification helps in understanding the plant's characteristics and its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the same family.

However, the effectiveness and safety of Gentianella pygmaea as a medicinal plant have not been extensively scientifically studied.
